Luigi Mangione won't face death penalty after judge dismisses charge
data.description:
STORY: Luigi Mangione will not face the death penalty.

That's after a federal judge on Friday dismissed murder and weapons charges against the accused killer of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

"We're all very relieved."

Mangione's lawyer Karen Friedman Agnifilo said her client and his legal team were gratified by the decision.

U.S. District Judge Margaret Garnett in Manhattan said she dropped the murder charge because it was legally incompatible with two stalking charges Mangione still faces.

Former federal prosecutor Neama Rahmani explains.

"The government argued that Louis G. Mangione committed the murder with a firearm in furtherance of stalking. The defense argued that stalking was not a crime of violence because stalking can be committed without force. Judge Garnet agreed, and because stalking was not a crime of violence, the judge dismissed the murder charges. And because the murder charge was the only charge that carried the potential death penalty, the judge struck the capital sentence in the case."

Twenty-seven-year-old Mangione still faces possible life in prison without the possibility of parole if convicted on the stalking charges.

Also, he still faces murder charges in a separate case brought by state prosecutors.

But the ruling in the federal case is a major blow to prosecutors, who have not decided whether to appeal the decision.

"The Department of Justice probably only charged Mangione because New York doesn't have the death penalty. It abolished it more than 20 years ago. But that's not a good legal reason necessarily to charge Mangione in federal court when it's already been charged by state prosecutors. What they were trying to do is fit the facts of this case into the federal murder statutes. And there was just no clear match. It was like forcing a square peg into a round hole."

Thompson, who led UnitedHealth Group's health insurance business, was shot and killed on December 4, 2024 outside the Hilton hotel in midtown Manhattan.

Mangione pleaded not guilty to all charges stemming from Thompson's death, and has been jailed since his arrest in Pennsylvania five days after the killing.

Jury selection for his trial is expected to begin in September.

China cuts proposed tariffs in EU dairy probe, industry groups say
data.description:
PARIS, Jan 30 (Reuters) - China has reduced proposed tariffs on certain dairy products from the European Union as it concludes an anti-subsidy investigation widely seen as retaliation for EU levies on Chinese electric cars, two European industry associations said.

In final tariffs communicated to the European side, China is proposing additional duties of up to 11.7%, compared with a maximum rate of 42.7% in provisional duties announced in December, the European Dairy Association (EDA) and Eucolait told Reuters. Many of the companies would be subject to a 9.5% rate, they said.

The European Commission and the Chinese Ministry of Commerce could not be immediately reached outside working hours.

* The dairy probe covers products including cream andcheese, and follows scrutiny of b randy and pork * Reduced rates would make it difficult for EU products to compete inChina, Alexander Anton, the EDA's secretary general , said. "It's a success but you're still out of the game."  * China imported $589 million of EU dairy products coveredby the current investigation in 2024 * Higher tariffs on EU imports could favour other foreignsuppliers like New Zealand * It could also bring relief for local Chinese producers grappling with a glut of milk and falling pricesagainst a backdrop of sluggish demand

(Reporting by Gus Trompiz;Additional reporting by Philip BlenkinsopEditing by Sudip Kar-Gupta)

data.title: Vinhomes Joint Stock Company Reports Earnings Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Vinhomes Joint Stock Company reported earnings results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. For the fourth quarter, the company reported revenue was VND 103,009,532 million compared to VND 32,413,479 million a year ago. Net income was VND 26,726,226 million compared to VND 12,159,464 million a year ago.
For the full year, revenue was VND 154,102,382 million compared to VND 102,323,186 million a year ago. Net income was VND 41,107,147 million compared to VND 31,801,258 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was VND 10,008 compared to VND 7,348 a year ago.

data.title: Nomura Holdings, Inc. Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Nomura Holdings, Inc. reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported revenue was JPY 1,227,928 million compared to JPY 1,197,938 million a year ago. Net income was JPY 91,553 million compared to JPY 101,441 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was JPY 31.21 compared to JPY 34.32 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was JPY 30.19 compared to JPY 33.08 a year ago.
For the nine months, revenue was JPY 3,545,729 million compared to JPY 3,657,389 million a year ago. Net income was JPY 576,392 million compared to JPY 268,766 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was JPY 195.46 compared to JPY 90.95 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was JPY 189.34 compared to JPY 87.66 a year ago.

data.title: National Aluminium Company Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
National Aluminium Company Limited reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was INR 47,309.5 million compared to INR 46,622.2 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 49,250.1 million compared to INR 47,613.1 million a year ago. Net income was INR 15,951.5 million compared to INR 15,663.2 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.69 compared to INR 8.53 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.69 compared to INR 8.53 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was INR 128,302.3 million compared to INR 115,198 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 132,992.7 million compared to INR 117,512 million a year ago. Net income was INR 40,745.7 million compared to INR 32,007.1 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 22.19 compared to INR 17.43 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 22.19 compared to INR 17.43 a year ago.

data.title: Hana Financial Group Inc. announces an Equity Buyback.
data.description:
Hana Financial Group Inc. (KOSE:A086790) announces a share repurchase program. Under the program, the company will repurchase up to KRW 200,000 million worth of its shares. The purpose behind the program is shareholder return and enhancement of corporate value.

The program will continue until April 30, 2026. As of January 29, 2026, the company had 8,298,066 shares in treasury through buyback within dividend capacity and had no shares in treasury through other acquisitions.

Power Grid Corporation of India Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Power Grid Corporation of India Limited reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was INR 123,950.9 million compared to INR 112,330.3 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 125,990.8 million compared to INR 117,430.6 million a year ago. Net income was INR 41,849.6 million compared to INR 38,616.3 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 4.5 compared to INR 4.15 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 4.5 compared to INR 4.15 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was INR 350,672.6 million compared to INR 335,169.7 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 357,137.4 million compared to INR 348,685.8 million a year ago. Net income was INR 113,816.2 million compared to INR 113,785.7 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 12.24 compared to INR 12.23 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 12.24 compared to INR 12.23 a year ago.

data.title: Chevron Signals More Venezuelan Heavy Crude in U.S. Refineries -- OPIS
data.description:
Chevron could increase Venezuelan heavy crude flows in its U.S. refining system, Chief Executive Officer Mike Wirth said on the company's fourth-quarter 2025 earnings call Friday.

The company currently processes about 50,000 b/d at its Pascagoula, Miss., refinery and could run an additional 100,000 b/d across its Gulf Coast refineries and on the West Coast at El Segundo in California, he added.

Chevron has increased Venezuelan crude production in its joint ventures by about 200,000 b/d since 2022, including deliveries into U.S. refineries. The refiner said production could grow by up to 50% over the next 18-24 months.

The company's downstream operations are well-positioned as California refineries close, Wirth said, citing the state's geographic and logistical isolation and fuel specification constraints, which contribute to higher local fuel prices and stronger refining margins. Chevron also reported its highest U.S. refinery throughput in two decades, reflecting recent expansions and improved operational efficiency.


This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.


Reporting by Allegra Fradkin, afradkin@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly, mkelly@opisnet.com
